findings.summary: All 8 sub-composition mounts are missing class="clip", data-width, and data-height — studio will silently load nothing (duration shows 0:00/0:00)
findings.evidence: Lines 198-244: scene-marker-wrap, scene-string-literal-wrap, scene-chart-wrap, scene-stat-wrap, scene-compare-wrap, scene-process-flow-wrap, scene-code-block-wrap, scene-cta-wrap all have data-composition-src and data-composition-id but lack class="clip", data-width="1080", and data-height="1920". Each child file's html body is 1080x1920 (confirmed via grep). data-duration is also absent on all 8 mounts (the child timelines use tl.set({},{},N) to own their lengths, ranging 11.0s to 22.5s — but the parent mounts must still declare class="clip" and dimensions for the studio to wire the slot).
findings.fix: Add class="clip" data-width="1080" data-height="1920" to each of the 8 scene-*-wrap divs. Do NOT add data-duration (the child's internal timeline owns its length per sub-composition-wiring.md). Example for scene-marker-wrap:

findings.summary: Root composition duration (127.5s) is shorter than narration.wav (127.7s) by 0.2s — MP4 will cut off mid-sentence
findings.evidence: npx hyperframes compositions reports main = 127.5s. ffprobe reports narration.wav = 127.704014s. Delta = 0.204s which exceeds the 0.0s safety margin (rule threshold is 0.5s but composition must be >= narration). The tl.to({},{duration:0.01},129.99) extender sets TOTAL_DURATION=130 for the root timeline but the audio element has data-duration="127.5" which is what hyperframes compositions reads as the clip boundary.
findings.fix: The TOTAL_DURATION constant in index.html is already 130 and tl.to({},{duration:0.01},129.99) pads to 130s. The issue is the audio element: <audio data-duration="127.5"> should be data-duration="128" (round up to cover 127.704s). Alternatively keep 127.5 but confirm hyperframes compositions is reading the audio clip boundary not the tl extender — if the render uses the tl.to() extender at 130s this may be a display artifact only. Safest fix: bump data-duration on the audio to 128 and verify with npx hyperframes compositions after change.

findings.summary: #cta-url "Long-form: ast-grep deep-dive →" — 1.3:1 contrast ratio, needs 3:1 for AA
findings.evidence: npx hyperframes validate reported: #cta-url "Long-form: ast-grep deep-dive →" — 1.3:1 (need 3:1, t=117s). This is a UI link element (not decorative text), so AA applies. Current color likely near-white text on a light cream background or vice versa.
findings.fix: Darken the text color of #cta-url to achieve at least 3:1 against its background. For the editorial cream palette (--bg ≈ #F5F0E8), use the terracotta accent (--accent ≈ #C4502A) or near-black (--text ≈ #1C1A18) which both clear 4.5:1 AA. Run npx hyperframes validate to confirm after change.

findings.summary: span.ribbon "ast-grep" — 1.17:1 contrast ratio, needs 3:1 for AA
findings.evidence: npx hyperframes validate reported: span.ribbon "ast-grep" — 1.17:1 (need 3:1, t=65s). The ribbon marker highlight element has near-identical foreground and background colors, making the text inside it illegible.
findings.fix: The .ribbon span text color needs to contrast at least 3:1 against the ribbon background color. If the ribbon background is terracotta (--accent), use white (#FFFFFF) or the cream bg (#F5F0E8) for the text — both clear 3.5:1+ against typical terracotta. If the ribbon is cream-on-cream, switch text to --text (near-black). Run npx hyperframes validate to confirm after change.
